other_observers: none with me; first found by JB Hayes
specific_location: Plum Creek delta area of Chatfield State Park
description: the bird was a small plover, significantly smaller than nearby Killdeer. The overall shape was quite rotund, with short legs, very short neck, round body shape, and very small bill contributing to this look. The bill was tiny, quite short and thin but blunt-tipped, and black. The eye was dark. The legs were quite short for a shorebird, and orange.  The bird had a pale sandy colored "helmet". The throat was white and there was a white color that seperated the sandy colored helmet and the sandy colored upperparts. The upperparts were uniform and the same color as the head. The bird had a half-collar that was also the same color as the upperparts. The remainder of the underparts were clean white.Behaviors: the bird was foraging on mudflats, and did not seem to be in association with any nearby shorebirdsCall: none heardPlumage: unknown but suspect a juvenal
